title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: 76ers center Joel Embiid will undergo surgery for appendicitis, missing Thursday's game against the Rockets.
Embiid's recovery timeline is unclear; the 76ers are in seventh place in the Eastern Conference with five games left.
Embiid is averaging 26.9 points, 7.7 rebounds, and 3.9 assists in 38 games this season, nearing the playoffs.

title: State commits $4M to address substance use crisis at Mass. and Cass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: State commits $4 million to address substance use crisis at Massachusetts Avenue and Melnea Cass Boulevard, focusing on housing and treatment.
Funding includes $2.24 million for housing support, $650,000 for clinical staffing, and $500,000 for diversion programs in Boston.
Recent data shows a 38% decrease in opioid-related deaths in Boston, the lowest since 2015, amid ongoing efforts to tackle the crisis.

title: Family of Stephenson King Jr., the man killed by Boston police officer now charged with manslaughter, demands transparency
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Stephenson King Jr. was fatally shot by Boston Police Officer Nicholas O'Malley in March, leading to a manslaughter charge.
King's family, represented by lawyer Ben Crump, demands the release of body-camera footage for transparency and accountability.
Officer O'Malley, on paid leave, has pleaded not guilty, with a defense fund raising over $500,000 for his legal expenses.

title: Her first marathon is for her dad and grandfather — lifelong South Boston residents
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Olivia Dennett, 22, from Wilmington, will run her first marathon in the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Dennett is running to honor her late father and grandfather, lifelong residents of South Boston.
She will support South Boston Neighborhood House, celebrating the community her family has cherished for generations.

title: She’s running Boston for her mom and cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sophie Goad, 27, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Goad's fundraising goal is $15,124, part of the team's $8.75 million target for cancer research.
The funds raised support the Claudia Adams Barr Program, advancing innovative basic cancer research.

title: Jaylen Brown says underwater training with a surfing legend helped him deal with anxiety
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown credits underwater training with surfing legend Laird Hamilton for helping manage anxiety and pressure.
Underwater training involves exercises like using heavy weights to simulate being underwater, helping Brown improve his mental resilience.
The training contributed to Brown's career-high season average of 28.8 points, aiding Celtics to secure a playoff berth.

title: Police search for owner, dog after man and dog are attacked in Everett
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Police in Everett seek dog owner after an attack on man and dog at 393 Ferry St.
The injured dog, Mushroom, required multiple surgeries and is recovering at BluePearl in Charlestown.
The owner of the attacking dog left the scene prior to the arrival of first responders.

title: Mass. House passes bill restricting teen social media and phones in schools
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts House approved a bill banning phones in schools and requiring parental consent for social media access for teens.
Under the bill, under-14s cannot use social media; 14-15-year-olds need parental permission; no limits for those 16 and older.
The proposal suggests age verification systems and updated school policies, now headed to the Senate for further consideration.

title: How Boston.com readers feel about the Boston Marathon not making this list of best marathons
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: 77% of 98 Boston.com readers believe Boston Marathon should be on USA Today's best marathons list.
Boston Marathon, known for its history and challenging course, missed USA Today's best marathons in America list.
This year's Boston Marathon is scheduled for April 20, despite its absence from the recent USA Today list.

title: Increase in bear encounters prompts new requirements for White Mountains visitors
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Starting May 1, Pemigewasset Wilderness visitors must use bear-resistant canisters for food and scented items.
The U.S. Forest Service noted increased bear encounters due to improperly stored food in the White Mountain National Forest.
Free bear-resistant canister loan programs are available in several New Hampshire locations, including Lincoln Woods Trailhead Cabin.
